title: Soutzoukakia
subtitle: Greek cumin meatballs in tomato sauce
excerpt: Soutzoukakia are elongated Greek meatballs strongly flavoured with cumin and garlic, browned in a pan and then simmered in a thick tomato sauce. They have a warm, spiced character that sets them apart from standard meatballs. Serve over rice or with bread.
sourceNotes: Soutzoukakia are associated with Smyrna (modern-day Izmir in Turkey) and the Greek communities that lived there before the population exchanges of the early twentieth century. They are a signature dish of what is sometimes called 'refugee cuisine' in Greece, brought by those who arrived in Greece from Asia Minor.

recipe.freezeNotes: Cool and freeze the meatballs in their sauce. Reheat gently on the hob, adding a splash of water.
recipe.batchNotes: Doubles easily. The sauce and meatballs keep in the fridge for 3 days.

content.text: Soutzoukakia are elongated spiced meatballs, strongly cumin-flavoured, browned and then simmered in a thick tomato and red wine sauce. They are richer and more aromatic than standard meatballs, the cumin giving them a distinctive warm depth that makes them stand out. Over rice or with bread they make a complete, filling meal.

items.prepNote: soaked in 2 tbsp red wine for 5 minutes

content.text: Mix the beef mince, soaked breadcrumbs, garlic, {{cumin-ground}} of ground cumin, {{cinnamon-ground}} of ground cinnamon, beaten egg, salt, and pepper in a large bowl. Mix well with your hands. Shape into oval sausage shapes about 7 cm long. You should get about 16.

Heat {{olive-oil}} in a large frying pan over medium-high heat. Brown the meatballs in batches, turning carefully, for about 6 minutes until golden on all sides. Transfer to a plate. Don't cook through at this stage; they will finish in the sauce.

Add {{red-wine}} of red wine to the same pan and let it bubble for 1 minute, scraping up any stuck bits. Add the tinned tomatoes, {{tomato-puree}} of tomato purée, and the cinnamon stick. Season with salt and pepper. Simmer for 5 minutes.

Return the meatballs to the sauce. Cover and simmer over low heat for 20 minutes, turning once, until the sauce is thick and the meatballs are cooked through. Remove the cinnamon stick before serving.

items.symptom: The meatballs break apart when turned
items.cause: Handled too early before a crust has formed, or mixture too soft
items.fix: Leave the meatballs in the pan without moving them for at least 2 minutes before attempting to turn. They release from the pan once they have a proper crust.
Remove the lid and simmer for a further 10 minutes. The sauce should coat the back of a spoon.

content.text: Soutzoukakia are a Greek-Turkish heritage dish brought to mainland Greece by communities displaced from Asia Minor in the early twentieth century. They are now a staple of Greek home cooking, appearing at family tables and in tavernas. Serve over rice with a green salad alongside.
